mnemonics.title: User Management Sequence
mnemonics.content: CAPD: Create (CREATE USER) -> Assign (GRANT) -> Privilege Changes (FLUSH PRIVILEGES) -> Delete (REVOKE then DROP USER)

mnemonics.title: Backup-Restore Commands
mnemonics.content: mysqldump OUT, mysql IN - Think of data flowing OUT to file, IN from file to database

acronyms.expansion: Database Administrator - manages database operations, security, and performance
Grant Rights And Privileges - gives specific permissions to users
Revoke Objects and Key Entitlements - removes previously granted permissions

visualTips.visualization: Think of username and hostname as a lock and key - the user 'john'@'localhost' can only unlock from the local machine, while 'john'@'%' can unlock from anywhere
Picture reloading a ammunition clip - FLUSH reloads the privilege settings into active memory
The > arrow points OUT like an exit - database data goes OUT to a file. Restoration uses < pointing IN like an entrance.

analogies.analogy: GRANT is like giving someone a key to your house, REVOKE is taking that key back
Like restarting a computer to apply new system settings - changes don't take effect until reloaded
Like taking a photograph (snapshot) of all your furniture - it captures the exact state at that moment
